mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 6.5% signals tightening labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings visibility, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in 70%+ of instances where UNRATE declines below 6.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts (HOUST) and real retail sales (RSAFS) indicates strong consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate revenue expectations.
failure_condition: 60d forward returns show negative correlation with the composite HOUST-RSAFS growth index (Rank IC < -0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 102.0 signals strengthening manufacturing activity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through improved corporate earnings momentum.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns decline when INDPRO exceeds 102.0 in 60%+ of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly core inflation reading (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% triggers central bank policy tightening expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through increased equity discount rates.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5% in 70%+ of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth (GDPC1) decline below 2.0% signals economic contraction risks, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through reduced corporate profit expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns show positive correlation with GDPC1 declines (Rank IC > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll growth (PAYEMS) decline below 100k signals weakening labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending momentum, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain negative when PAYEMS declines below 100k in 60%+ of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent rise in industrial production (INDPRO) and decline in unemployment (UNRATE) indicates synchronized supply-side expansion and labor market strength, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through improved earnings visibility.
failure_condition: 5d forward returns show negative correlation with the composite INDPRO-UNRATE signal (Rank IC < -0.05).
